text: The concept of voluntary attention sits at the intersection of psychology, philosophy of mind, and ethics. For James, it was not merely a cognitive tool but a moral one. The ability to choose where one places attention is the ability to choose, in some meaningful sense, what one's life consists of. A life of involuntary attention — always hijacked by the loudest signal — is a life shaped by accident rather than intention. James believed this made attentional training the deepest form of self-cultivation available to a human being. To master attention is to author the content of experience itself. Few capacities, he thought, were more worth developing.
question: How did James characterize a life governed entirely by involuntary attention?

options.text: A life rich in sensory experience but lacking in depth
A life shaped by accident rather than intention
A life well-adapted to high-stimulus environments
A life driven by immediate rather than derived interest
